records.ety: [Originally, to assent to as true; OE. soðien to verify, AS. gesōðian to prove the truth of, to bear witness. See Sooth, a.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Soothed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Soothing.]

defs: To assent to; to comply with; to gratify; to humor by compliance; to please with blandishments or soft words; to flatter.
quotes: Good, my lord, soothe him, let him take the fellow.
I've tried the force of every reason on him, Soothed and caressed, been angry, soothed again.

defs: To assuage; to mollify; to calm; to comfort; as, to soothe a crying child; to soothe one's sorrows.
quotes: Music hath charms to soothe the savage breast, To soften rocks, or bend a knotted oak.
Though the sound of Fame May for a moment soothe, it can not slake The fever of vain longing.
